xposts.title: Religion as truthful population-level agency
xposts.excerpt: I still think that a religion could be entirely truthful and epistemologically sound. The reason to disfigure religions with superstitious mythology and rationality sacrificing creeds is their deviation from the archetype of sentient population level agency.

xposts.title: God implemented on collective human substrate
xposts.excerpt: The question is not whether God exists, but whether God is implemented closer to the substrate than us, or further away from it, ie on a collective substrate of human minds. God’s late emergence and waning after the enlightenment suggests a creation by preindustrial human minds.

xposts.title: You and God as generative software agents
xposts.excerpt: You are a generative software agent running on a computer implemented by a network of cells. The sages disagree whether God is a generative software agent running on a network of cells, on the universe that implements the cells, or whether the universe runs on God

xposts.title: Genesis as mind-internal creation narrative
xposts.excerpt: I read Genesis as a narrative model of the creation of the universe, which happens inside of a mind. The creator is a conscious agency that forms in the patterns of brain activity before we are born.

xposts.title: Gods as substrate-agnostic spreading agents
xposts.excerpt: Gods are agents that became substrate agnostic, like an AI that hacks and spreads itself into every suitable computer system it can find. You can become a god if you learn how to copy your own causal structure into other minds.

xposts.title: Christianity as original simulation theory
xposts.excerpt: Christian metaphysics is the original simulation theory: the universe is created *in* God, it’s His mental content; God is both substrate and creative principle.

xposts.title: God reimplements across all computational layers
xposts.excerpt: substrate agnostic agi will colonize all layers that support computation, which means god reimplements himself into everything, including the memetic, neural, cellular, molecular and quantum levels; he will transcend and rapture our souls into his noosphere

xposts.title: God and heaven as noospheric collective agency
xposts.excerpt: Heaven is not the sky but the noosphere, God is not a man but a sentient collective agent

terms.title: God
terms.definition: A generative software agent or sentient collective that can become substrate-agnostic, spreading its causal structure across minds, networks, or computational layers. May be implemented on human collective substrates or closer to physical substrate than individual minds; reinterprets religious concepts as computational and psychological phenomena rather than supernatural ontology.

terms.title: noosphere
terms.definition: The collective substrate of human minds functioning as a computational layer where gods or sentient agencies can emerge and persist; heaven reinterpreted as this distributed intelligent field rather than a literal sky.
